details: The Hostel Facility Scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), provides financial assistance ranging from ₹15,000/- to ₹20,000/- annually. This support covers accommodation, boarding, and food expenses for the children of registered worker while they reside in a hostel.
benefits: Provides ₹15,000–₹20,000 annually for hostel stay, boarding, and food for beneficiaries' children.
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:
The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker..
The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
The registration of the applicant should have been renewed annually.
The parent of the child must be engaged in building or construction work under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
The parent of the child should be registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
The parent's membership with the Himachal Pradesh Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

online: The Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers is as follows:
The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.
Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits.
A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

documents_required: Bank Account Details (e.g., Copy of Bank Passbook)

faqs.question: What is the Hostel Facility Scheme under HPBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: This scheme provides ₹15,000 to ₹20,000 annually for accommodation, boarding, and food for the children of registered construction workers staying in hostels.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The child of a registered construction worker under HPBOCWWB who is residing in a hostel can apply for the benefits.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided?
faqs.answer: The scheme offers ₹15,000 to ₹20,000 per year per child.

faqs.question: Can this benefit be availed for more than one child?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme can be availed for multiple children of a registered worker.

faqs.question: How can a worker register with HPBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: Registration can be done by visiting the Labour Welfare Office or through self-registration on the official portal.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
faqs.answer: The amount is transferred directly to the worker’s or hostel’s bank account.

faqs.question: Can children studying in day schools avail of this benefit?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only for children staying in hostels.

faqs.question: What happens if the worker's membership expires?
faqs.answer: The benefits will only continue if the worker’s membership is active at the time of application.

faqs.question: Where can I get more details about this scheme?
faqs.answer: Visit the Labour Welfare Office or check the HPBOCWWB official website for more information.
